mainEntity.name: When does the date of injury start for Utah occupational hearing loss claims?
mainEntity.text: When does the date of injury start for Utah occupational hearing loss claims?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah Code 34A-2-504 sets the date as last harmful noise exposure or direct head injury, starting statutes of limitations and benefit calculations from that point.

suggestedAnswer.text: For gradual NIHL, 'date of injury' is last workday with harmful exposure, crucial for timely filing (typically 1-3 years SOL). Head trauma cases use incident date. Delays from delayed awareness don't extend; report promptly. Murray workers in noisy jobs must track exposure end dates for claims.[4]

name: Date of Injury for Gradual Hearing Loss Claims
description: Utah Code 34A-2-504 sets the date as last harmful noise exposure or direct head injury, starting statutes of limitations and benefit calculations from that poin
